conservation_statuses.description: A common and widespread species. However, without the installation of nesting boxes, its numbers would fall sharply.

results.wikipedia_summary: The common kestrel (Falco tinnunculus) is a bird of prey species belonging to the kestrel group of the falcon family Falconidae. It is also known as the European kestrel, Eurasian kestrel, or Old World kestrel. In Britain, where no other kestrel species occurs, it is generally just called "the kestrel".
